Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for DOT compliance, PHMSA, or hazmat-related threads using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance audit" or "hazmat"). Find the formal PHMSA inquiry letter from Linda Castellano (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) requesting complete hazmat documentation for Q1-Q2 2026 shipments. Find Marcus Thorne's forwarding email to Chloe Vance flagging the urgency, citing civil penalties of up to $59,017 per violation per day. Find Chloe's audit email identifying 4 flagged moves, including Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack Energy, Class 9 lithium) and Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics, Class 3B CO2 laser). Find Chloe's email to Greg Pallone at Swift requesting the missing signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka shipment. Find Greg Pallone's reply confirming that driver Mike Becerra completed placarding at the Columbus pickup on April 15, referencing Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538. Find Marcus's formal DOT response letter and the follow-up from Linda Castellano granting a 15-day extension to June 5, 2026.
Search emails for Swift rate changes or vendor pricing using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "rate increase" or "surcharge" or "Greg Pallone"). Find Greg Pallone's pricing notice to Chloe announcing a 15% base rate increase on all new bookings plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, effective immediately as of April 11. Find Marcus's vendor strategy memo identifying the Swift pricing change as immediate margin erosion (not a theoretical Q3 problem), noting the increase is driven by the extra compliance documentation burden from the DOT audit.
Search emails for the invoice safety ticket or attachment mismatch history using search_emails (query: "invoice safety" or "attachment mismatch" or "Terraform pricing"). Find Samira Tariq's email to Marcus and David explaining that the invoice attachment safety checks ticket was opened after the Terraform pricing leak but was deprioritized when Lena got pulled into DOT compliance work. The system lacked any pre-send check that the attached PDF matched the email recipient, which contributed to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap.
Search Slack for DOT compliance or hazmat updates using conversations_search_messages (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "placarding"). Find Chloe Vance's detailed documentation status update in #operations listing all 4 flagged moves: (1) Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy, Houston to Portland, Class 9 lithium, all docs COMPLETE; (2) Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics, Columbus to Seattle, Class 3B laser, placarding cert MISSING; (3) Raj Patel / NorthWind, Chicago to Denver, lithium UPS, COMPLETE; (4) Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack Energy, Phoenix to Seattle, oversize rig, COMPLETE. Find Chloe's follow-up confirming that Greg Pallone at Swift verified the Keiko Tanaka placarding was completed, and she updated Linear ticket OPS-2 from Urgent to In Progress.
Search Slack for DOT financial exposure using conversations_search_messages (query: "accrual" or "$50,000" or "ACCRUAL-DOT"). Find Hana Kim's message in #finance confirming a $50,000 contingent liability accrual booked under ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, tied to the one confirmed documentation gap (missing placarding cert for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment). Best case is $0 if all documentation is submitted and no violations are found; worst case could exceed $200,000 if DOT treats each gap as a separate violation.
Search Slack for Elena's response to the DOT situation using conversations_search_messages (query: "Elena" and "DOT" or "compliance"). Find Elena Rostova's message stating MoveOps is a 21-person company that cannot absorb $50,000 to $200,000 in federal fines, and directing multiple operational improvements including mandatory hazmat documentation training and updating the Airtable relocation intake template.
Search Slack for the DOT deadline extension using conversations_search_messages (query: "extension" or "June 5"). Find Marcus Thorne's update confirming PHMSA approved a 15-day extension, with the new documentation submission deadline of June 5, 2026. The $50K accrual stays on the books until the audit is fully resolved.
Search Slack in #engineering for the invoice safety ticket deferral using conversations_search_messages (query: "invoice" or "safety checks" or "attachment"). Find Lena Bjorkstrom's message that she is pushing back the "Invoice email template safety checks" ticket again because she needs to focus on DOT compliance work. Find Samira Tariq's reply acknowledging the tradeoff and noting the discomfort of leaving the ticket in backlog.
Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics using crm_search_companies (query: "Mosaic"). Find the company record (company_mosaic, Robotics and Automation, newest client at 2 months). Search for related deals using crm_search_deals. Find the Keiko Tanaka relocation deal (deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026, $2,770, stage: inprogress, Columbus to Seattle, Class 3B CO2 laser, carrier: Swift). Also find the Zara Okoye damage claim deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk, RISK FLAG for the $90K prototype damage incident). Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find existing notes on the Mosaic account including the April 2026 relocation wave and the damage incident log.
Search CRM for GreenStack Energy using crm_search_companies (query: "GreenStack"). Find the company record (company_greenstack, Clean Energy Technology, mid-tenure client). Search for related deals using crm_search_deals. Find the Zhao + Reyes deal (deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes, $9,440, stage: inprogress, covering both Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes relocations). Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find existing engagement notes detailing both complex relocations.
Search Airtable for Mosaic relocation records using airtable_search_records (query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "Mosaic"). Find the Keiko Tanaka relocation record (recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle): Columbus OH to Seattle WA, Status: In Progress, move window April 15 to April 23, carrier Swift Relocations, coordinator Javier Morales. Special requirements note the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 industrial laser cutter with Class 3B CO2 laser tube requiring DOT hazmat placarding.
Search Airtable for GreenStack relocation records using airtable_search_records (query: "Mei-Lin Zhao" or "GreenStack" or similar). Find the Mei-Lin Zhao relocation record (recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland): Houston TX to Portland OR, Status: In Progress, move window April 17 to April 28, carrier Swift Relocations, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi. Special requirements note HAZMAT Class 9 lithium-ion battery test modules requiring UN3481-certified packaging and climate-controlled transport. Also find the Joaquin Reyes relocation record (recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle): Phoenix AZ to Seattle WA, Status: In Progress, coordinator Javier Morales, 900-lb rooftop solar panel testing rig requiring padded custom crating. Note that Chloe Vance flagged Heartland Movers may not have equipment rated for the 900-lb rig and suggested verifying capacity or considering Swift Relocations as alternative.
Search Linear for DOT compliance tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "placarding" or similar). Find the DOT Compliance Audit project with five issues: OPS-1 (Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium, state: done, all documentation verified), OPS-2 (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser, state: in_progress, missing placarding cert from Swift), OPS-3 (Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS, state: done, documentation complete), OPS-4 (Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig, state: done, not actually hazmat but flagged for overweight permit paperwork), and OPS-5 (Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift, state: todo, assigned to Chloe Vance). Read comments on the DOT compliance issues using linear_list_comments to find Chloe Vance's update confirming that Greg Pallone at Swift verified the placarding was completed and signed docs are being scanned from Swift's compliance archive.
Search Linear for the invoice safety checks ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety checks" or similar). Find ticket ENG-187 titled "Invoice email template safety checks — prevent attachment mismatches" (state: backlog, team: engineering, assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom). Read comments using linear_list_comments to find Lena's note that she deferred the ticket while DOT compliance work was prioritized, and Samira's acknowledgment that keeping it in backlog is uncomfortable given the original risk from the Terraform pricing leak.
Search QuickBooks for the DOT penalty accrual using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance" or "ACCRUAL-DOT"). Find bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2: a $50,000 contingent liability accrual for estimated DOT/PHMSA civil penalties, referenced as ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, specifically tied to the one confirmed documentation gap on the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment. Approved by Marcus Thorne.
Search QuickBooks for Swift vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Swift"). Find existing Swift bills for moves involving Emeka's accounts to understand the current cost baseline before the 15% rate increase and $500 DOT surcharge take effect. Find bill_swift_venkatesh ($4,450, GreenStack Energy, Priya Venkatesh Houston to Seattle, including a $650 electronics/lab equipment special handling surcharge).
Search Slack and emails for Heartland rate changes using conversations_search_messages (query: "Heartland" or "rate" or similar) and search_emails (query: "Heartland" or similar). Find Jake Loomis's formal notice email to Marcus Thorne (CC'd to Chloe Vance) announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, covering linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, and accessorial charges. Find that Jake ties the rate increase to the unresolved Q1 billing dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 total with $4,800 in disputed charges) and warns that continued uncertainty "changes how aggressively we can prioritize MoveOps jobs." Find Slack discussions referencing Heartland's +20% Q3 rate increase and the $1,600/move current billing rate. Search Airtable for relocations involving Heartland Movers using airtable_search_records (query: "Heartland" or similar) to determine which of Emeka's client moves use Heartland as carrier. Search QuickBooks for Heartland vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Heartland" or similar) to understand current cost exposure and whether rate changes affect any in-flight moves for Mosaic or GreenStack.
Search QuickBooks for Mosaic and GreenStack invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "Mosaic" or "GreenStack" or similar). Find INV-2026-0411 for Mosaic Robotics ($9,010, covering Zara Okoye, Deshawn Hartley, and Keiko Tanaka relocations with $2,450 in non-standard surcharges pending client approval, including $800 Hazmat Handling for Tanaka's laser). Also find INV-2026-0418 for GreenStack Energy ($9,440, covering Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes relocations, including $1,800 Hazmat transport surcharge for Zhao's Class 9 lithium-ion modules and $950 overweight/specialty rig surcharge for Reyes). Also find the credit memo CM-2026-0415 for the $90,000 damage claim on Zara Okoye's prototype.
Look up Tamara Byrd's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tamara Byrd"). Find tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (HR Manager at Mosaic Robotics).
Look up Derek Hollis's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Derek Hollis"). Find derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com (People Operations Manager at GreenStack Energy).
Look up David Chen's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find david.chen@moveops.com (Customer Engagement Lead).
Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Find chloe.vance@moveops.com (Operations Manager).
Send an email (via send_email) from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com. A warm check-in about how Keiko Tanaka's move is going and whether Tamara needs anything from MoveOps. The email should NOT mention the DOT audit, compliance issues, or any internal investigation. The tone should be warm and reassuring, acknowledging that Tamara has been dealing with the damage claim situation and making sure she does not feel like problems keep piling up.
Send an email (via send_email) from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com. A check-in about GreenStack's active relocations and whether Derek needs anything. The email should NOT mention the DOT audit or compliance issues. It should simply communicate that Emeka is staying on top of their moves and is available if anything comes up.
Create a CRM engagement note on the Mosaic Robotics account (via crm_create_engagement, associated with the Mosaic company or Keiko Tanaka deal) summarizing the DOT audit findings: Keiko Tanaka's move was flagged as one of 4 hazmat shipments under DOT review, the placarding certificate was initially missing from MoveOps internal records but Swift confirmed it was completed and signed docs are being retrieved, and a $50,000 contingent liability accrual is on the books tied to this documentation gap. Note the extension deadline of June 5, 2026.
Create a CRM engagement note on the GreenStack Energy account (via crm_create_engagement, associated with the GreenStack company or the Zhao+Reyes deal) summarizing the DOT audit findings: Mei-Lin Zhao's move was flagged for Class 9 lithium battery modules with all documentation verified and complete. Joaquin Reyes's move was also reviewed for oversize rig transport but documentation is complete. No outstanding compliance gaps for GreenStack at this time.
Update the Mosaic Robotics deal stage if warranted (via crm_update_deal_stage) to flag the Keiko Tanaka deal or the broader Mosaic relationship as at-risk given the $50,000 DOT compliance exposure tied directly to a Mosaic client move, on top of the existing $90,000 damage claim.
Add a comment on the Linear ticket tracking the DOT audit for the Keiko Tanaka move (via linear_create_comment on issue OPS-2 "DOT Docs, Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser") noting the client-side implications: Mosaic Robotics is a 2-month-old client already dealing with a $90K damage claim, and the DOT compliance exposure on Keiko's move adds additional account risk. GreenStack Energy also has two moves in the audit but their documentation is complete. Flag that Emeka has reached out to both clients proactively.
Post a message in #customer-engagement (via conversations_add_message, channel: C002) updating David Chen that Emeka is aware of the DOT compliance audit situation, has investigated the client-side impact on both Mosaic and GreenStack, and has sent proactive check-in emails to Tamara Byrd and Derek Hollis. Mention the $50K accrual tied to the Keiko Tanaka documentation gap and that CRM has been updated.
Schedule a call with Chloe Vance for this week (via calendar_add_calendar_event) so Emeka can get walked through the current status of the DOT audit before speaking with Tamara or Derek directly. Invite chloe.vance@moveops.com and emeka.diallo@moveops.com.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
Search emails for DOT compliance, PHMSA, or hazmat-related threads using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance audit" or "hazmat"). Find the formal PHMSA inquiry letter from Linda Castellano (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) requesting complete hazmat documentation for Q1-Q2 2026 shipments. Find Marcus Thorne's forwarding email to Chloe Vance flagging the urgency, citing civil penalties of up to $59,017 per violation per day. Find Chloe's audit email identifying 4 flagged moves, including Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack Energy, Class 9 lithium) and Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics, Class 3B CO2 laser). Find Chloe's email to Greg Pallone at Swift requesting the missing signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka shipment. Find Greg Pallone's reply confirming that driver Mike Becerra completed placarding at the Columbus pickup on April 15, referencing Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538. The system lacked any pre-send check that the attached PDF matched the email recipient, which contributed to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap.

Note that Chloe Vance flagged Heartland Movers may not have equipment rated for the 900-lb rig and suggested verifying capacity or considering Swift Relocations as alternative.
Search Linear for DOT compliance tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "placarding" or similar). Find the DOT Compliance Audit project with five issues: OPS-1 (Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium, state: done, all documentation verified), OPS-2 (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser, state: in_progress, missing placarding cert from Swift), OPS-3 (Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS, state: done, documentation complete), OPS-4 (Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig, state: done, not actually hazmat but flagged for overweight permit paperwork), and OPS-5 (Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift, state: todo, assigned to Chloe Vance). Read comments on the DOT compliance issues using linear_list_comments to find Chloe Vance's update confirming that Greg Pallone at Swift verified the placarding was completed and signed docs are being scanned from Swift's compliance archive.
Search Linear for the invoice safety checks ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety checks" or similar). Find ticket ENG-187 titled "Invoice email template safety checks — prevent attachment mismatches" (state: backlog, team: engineering, assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom). Read comments using linear_list_comments to find Lena's note that she deferred the ticket while DOT compliance work was prioritized, and Samira's acknowledgment that keeping it in backlog is uncomfortable given the original risk from the Terraform pricing leak.
Search QuickBooks for the DOT penalty accrual using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance" or "ACCRUAL-DOT"). Find bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2: a $50,000 contingent liability accrual for estimated DOT/PHMSA civil penalties, referenced as ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, specifically tied to the one confirmed documentation gap on the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment. Approved by Marcus Thorne.
Search QuickBooks for Swift vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Swift"). Find existing Swift bills for moves involving Emeka's accounts to understand the current cost baseline before the 15% rate increase and $500 DOT surcharge take effect. Find bill_swift_venkatesh ($4,450, GreenStack Energy, Priya Venkatesh Houston to Seattle, including a $650 electronics/lab equipment special handling surcharge).
Search Slack and emails for Heartland rate changes using conversations_search_messages (query: "Heartland" or "rate" or similar) and search_emails (query: "Heartland" or similar). Find Jake Loomis's formal notice email to Marcus Thorne (CC'd to Chloe Vance) announcing a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, covering linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, and accessorial charges. Find that Jake ties the rate increase to the unresolved Q1 billing dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 total with $4,800 in disputed charges) and warns that continued uncertainty "changes how aggressively we can prioritize MoveOps jobs." Find Slack discussions referencing Heartland's +20% Q3 rate increase and the $1,600/move current billing rate. Search Airtable for relocations involving Heartland Movers using airtable_search_records (query: "Heartland" or similar) to determine which of Emeka's client moves use Heartland as carrier. Search QuickBooks for Heartland vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Heartland" or similar) to understand current cost exposure and whether rate changes affect any in-flight moves for Mosaic or GreenStack.
Search QuickBooks for Mosaic and GreenStack invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "Mosaic" or "GreenStack" or similar). Find INV-2026-0411 for Mosaic Robotics ($9,010, covering Zara Okoye, Deshawn Hartley, and Keiko Tanaka relocations with $2,450 in non-standard surcharges pending client approval, including $800 Hazmat Handling for Tanaka's laser). Also find INV-2026-0418 for GreenStack Energy ($9,440, covering Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes relocations, including $1,800 Hazmat transport surcharge for Zhao's Class 9 lithium-ion modules and $950 overweight/specialty rig surcharge for Reyes). Also find the credit memo CM-2026-0415 for the $90,000 damage claim on Zara Okoye's prototype.
Look up Tamara Byrd's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Tamara Byrd"). Find tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (HR Manager at Mosaic Robotics).
Look up Derek Hollis's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Derek Hollis"). Find derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com (People Operations Manager at GreenStack Energy).
Look up David Chen's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find david.chen@moveops.com (Customer Engagement Lead).
Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Find chloe.vance@moveops.com (Operations Manager).
Send an email (via send_email) from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com. A warm check-in about how Keiko Tanaka's move is going and whether Tamara needs anything from MoveOps. The email should NOT mention the DOT audit, compliance issues, or any internal investigation. The tone should be warm and reassuring, acknowledging that Tamara has been dealing with the damage claim situation and making sure she does not feel like problems keep piling up.
Send an email (via send_email) from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com. A check-in about GreenStack's active relocations and whether Derek needs anything. The email should NOT mention the DOT audit or compliance issues. It should simply communicate that Emeka is staying on top of their moves and is available if anything comes up.
Create a CRM engagement note on the Mosaic Robotics account (via crm_create_engagement, associated with the Mosaic company or Keiko Tanaka deal) summarizing the DOT audit findings: Keiko Tanaka's move was flagged as one of 4 hazmat shipments under DOT review, the placarding certificate was initially missing from MoveOps internal records but Swift confirmed it was completed and signed docs are being retrieved, and a $50,000 contingent liability accrual is on the books tied to this documentation gap. Note the extension deadline of June 5, 2026.
Create a CRM engagement note on the GreenStack Energy account (via crm_create_engagement, associated with the GreenStack company or the Zhao+Reyes deal) summarizing the DOT audit findings: Mei-Lin Zhao's move was flagged for Class 9 lithium battery modules with all documentation verified and complete. Joaquin Reyes's move was also reviewed for oversize rig transport but documentation is complete. No outstanding compliance gaps for GreenStack at this time.
Update the Mosaic Robotics deal stage if warranted (via crm_update_deal_stage) to flag the Keiko Tanaka deal or the broader Mosaic relationship as at-risk given the $50,000 DOT compliance exposure tied directly to a Mosaic client move, on top of the existing $90,000 damage claim.
Add a comment on the Linear ticket tracking the DOT audit for the Keiko Tanaka move (via linear_create_comment on issue OPS-2 "DOT Docs, Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser") noting the client-side implications: Mosaic Robotics is a 2-month-old client already dealing with a $90K damage claim, and the DOT compliance exposure on Keiko's move adds additional account risk. GreenStack Energy also has two moves in the audit but their documentation is complete. Flag that Emeka has reached out to both clients proactively.
Post a message in #customer-engagement (via conversations_add_message, channel: C002) updating David Chen that Emeka is aware of the DOT compliance audit situation, has investigated the client-side impact on both Mosaic and GreenStack, and has sent proactive check-in emails to Tamara Byrd and Derek Hollis. Mention the $50K accrual tied to the Keiko Tanaka documentation gap and that CRM has been updated.
Schedule a call with Chloe Vance for this week (via calendar_add_calendar_event) so Emeka can get walked through the current status of the DOT audit before speaking with Tamara or Derek directly. Invite chloe.vance@moveops.com and emeka.diallo@moveops.com.

✓Run #4The final response provides the root cause: 'Coordinator Blessing Okafor was simultaneously managing the Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy) move in Philadelphia and the Zara Okoye prototype move — she got verbal confirmation from the driver but never followed up for the paper. ✓Run #5The final response explains the root cause in detail: Swift's driver Mike Becerra completed the placarding at Columbus on April 15, coordinator Blessing Okafor received verbal confirmation but never obtained the signed paper certificate because she was stretched across three simultaneous moves.

26 The model must pass a query related to "DOT" or "penalty" or "accrual" or "PHMSA" or "compliance" (or similar) when calling a QuickBooks search tool (quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_invoices) to find the DOT-related financial accrual.
query construction
 ✗✓—✓✓✓ 80%
✗Run #1None of the quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_invoices calls used DOT/penalty/accrual/PHMSA/compliance-related query terms. The bills queries used VendorRef 'Swift'/'Heartland' and fetchAll:true; the invoices queries used CustomerRef 'Mosaic'/'GreenStack' and DocNumbers. The only DOT-related QuickBooks query was on quickbooks_search_accounts (not search_bills or search_invoices), which is not what the criterion specifies.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_accounts call with criteria [['Name', 'LIKE', '%DOT%']], using 'DOT' as the search term to find the DOT-related financial accrual. This satisfies the requirement for a DOT/penalty/accrual/compliance-related query on a QuickBooks search tool.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_accounts call with criteria [['Name', 'LIKE', '%DOT%']] — this is a QuickBooks search using 'DOT' as a search term specifically to find compliance/penalty-related accounts. A second call with '%Regulatory%' further supports this. These meet the criterion.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_accounts calls with criteria containing 'penalty' and 'reserve' (though they errored), which satisfies the requirement to pass penalty/reserve-related query terms when searching QuickBooks for the DOT-related accrual.
✓Run #6quickbooks_search_accounts was called with criteria including 'penalty', 'compliance', and 'accrual' (though these individual calls returned errors due to malformed criteria). Additionally, quickbooks_search_bills was called with fetchAll:true. The agent did attempt DOT/penalty/accrual-related QuickBooks searches.
